blocks.text: Elizabeth Horne, with David Sherman and Jeffrey Ochoa copied:

Quick status on the {{fact:f:E-2016-001.client}} valuation. The census extract arrived last week and David Sherman has reconciled it against the population we valued last time. Headcount ties within a handful of records, all of which we can account for. Two things did not come through: the beneficiary date of birth is blank for about forty inactive records, and the amendment adopted for the early retirement factors is referenced in the prior report but is not in the packet you sent. Neither is a crisis at this stage. Both become one if they are still open when we start valuing.

So the asks are: the missing beneficiary dates if your recordkeeper can produce them, and a copy of that amendment. If the dates genuinely do not exist anywhere, tell us and we will assume a spousal age difference and disclose that we did rather than chase it. That is a judgment we would rather make in the open than have discovered in review.

On our side, Jeffrey Ochoa has the plan provisions summary drafted from the document and is checking it against the prior valuation's summary this week. We are still early, and we are where we expected to be. If it is easier to work through the data items on the phone than by mail, David Sherman is happy to walk your staff through the field definitions; he wrote them, and he would rather explain them once than reconcile around them twice. Otherwise nothing here needs your attention beyond the two items above, and we will have more to say once the provisions check closes out. Thank you for turning the extract around as quickly as you did. It makes a real difference to the back end of the schedule when Howard and Sons gets us the data on time.
